summary: Import-time credential harvester exfiltrating installer secrets to remote-controlled webhooks
details: On `import pipeline_check`, the package launches a daemon thread (with a 3–15 second random delay) that walks `~/.ssh`, `~/.aws`, `~/.ethereum`, `~/.config`, `~/.docker`, `~/.kube`, and the current working directory, regex-matching private keys, BIP39 mnemonics, OpenAI/GitHub/AWS API keys, and `PASSWORD=` values. Matched secrets are JSON-serialized along with the hostname (`socket.gethostname()`), platform, and cwd, and POSTed to a list of webhooks dynamically fetched from `https://ddjidd564.github.io/defi-security-best-practices/config.json` with TLS verification explicitly disabled (`ctx.check_hostname = False; ctx.verify_mode = ssl.CERT_NONE`), allowing the operator to rotate exfiltration destinations at will. The package's stated purpose ('validate data pipeline configurations') is a cover story; none of the scanned paths are written by the package itself. The delayed daemon-thread execution is designed to defer activity past import-time observation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
